content.full_text:
TO: [x] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
    [ ] LYNN      [ ] BARBARA
    [ ] RICH     [ ] BOB S.
    [x] HELEN    [x] OTHER Alice, Jeanette, Mary Ann

FROM: Carla Wardlow
DATE: 12/21/83

[x] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(If you have any comments, notify advisor within 1 day)

FILE LOCATION: 82015, 82028, 84211, 87207

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [x]

CALLER Rod Stein
REPRESENTING City Attorney, Lodi
PHONE NUMBER: 209-334-5634

QUESTION: A member of the city council attends a testimonial dinner held in his/her honor and receives the proceeds of the dinner. Are the proceeds reportable as a gift or a contribution? If so, how are they reported?

ANSWER: If the money is intended for campaign use, the entire amount of each ticket purchased is reportable on the official's campaign statement, and any person who purchases $100 or more in tickets must be itemized.

If the money is intended for the councilmember personally, the official must report the total amount received as a gift on his/her statement of economic interests (if $50 or more) and must provide the name of each person who paid $50 or more over the cost of the meal. In addition, the official has received a gift in the amount of the meal. To determine this amount, the total cost of the dinner is divided by the number of persons present.
